title: rubus hirtus subsp. minutidentatus
intro: Rubus hirtus subsp. minutidentatus, also known as the thorny blackberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus hirtus plant, belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) and characterized by its thorny stems and edible blackberries. The medicinal properties of Rubus hirtus subsp. minutidentatus include its high content of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which may help to reduce the risk of certain diseases, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. Traditionally, Rubus hirtus subsp. minutidentatus has been used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to support overall health and well-being.
